author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Comprehensive audit and bug-fix pass on the ideaslab-discovery-clean Python pipeline (Discovery Gap Sentinel). Found and fixed 3 blockers, 3 risks, and 4 nits across governance.py, check_run_scope.py, evidence.py, report_builder.py, and 4 files with stale comments. Also removed ~985 lines of dead render_html v1 code from report_builder.py and stubbed the evidence.py Gate 4 LLM wrapper functions. Test suite went from 229 to 359 passing. Pipeline health rating improved from 5/10 to 9/10.
problems: B1 (BLOCKER): governance.py sentence counter fired on abbreviation periods (Dr., Mr., Ms., etc.) causing valid 2-sentence narratives with titles to raise a false 'too many sentences' error

B2 (BLOCKER, fixed in earlier session): glossary.json values are dicts {definition, synonyms} but report_builder.py passed them raw to string formatters, causing dict-to-string coercion and broken output
B3 (BLOCKER, fixed in earlier session): .glossary CSS class was applied to the <details> wrapper in render_html_v2 instead of the <dl> element, breaking grid layout
R1 (RISK): evidence.py retry fallback prompt never asked for item_verdicts, so retry could not recover from 'item_verdicts missing keys' failures — second attempt always failed with same error
R2 (RISK): check_run_scope.py compared against all current git dirty files with no baseline, so pre-existing uncommitted changes were always flagged as session violations in a non-clean working tree
R3 (RISK): validate_report() in governance.py hardcoded 16-section check with no parameter and wrong return type annotation (-> None instead of -> list[str])
N1 (NIT): ~985 lines of dead render_html v1 function in report_builder.py (never called; render_from_results.py imports render_html_v2 as render_html)
N2 (NIT): ~530 lines of dead Gate 4 LLM wrapper functions in evidence.py (generate_session_narrative, analyze_section_with_llm, generate_followup_questions — all 0 callers in Gate 5 pipeline)
N3/N4 (NIT): stale comments in 4 files referencing Gate 2, Gate 4, retired scripts (qa_accuracy, apply_review_revisions, sanitize_analysis, recompute_question_status)
approaches: Fix B1: Added _ABBREVS pattern and _count_sentences() helper to governance.py that replaces abbreviation periods with a null-byte placeholder before splitting on sentence boundaries
Fix R3: Changed validate_report signature to (report: dict, *, expected_sections: int = 16) -> list[str]; updated render_from_results.py to pass expected_sections=len(registry)
Added 5 new tests to test_governance.py: 2 abbreviation tests, 2 parameterized expected_sections tests, 1 wrong-count-with-custom-expected test
Fix R2: Rewrote check_run_scope.py to support --snapshot FILE (records pre-session dirty paths) and --baseline FILE (compares only new violations). Updated agent spec Steps 1 and 6.
Fix R1: Updated evidence.py retry prompt to conditionally include item_verdicts key description (with exact required keys) when required_items is non-empty

Fix N3/N4: Updated 4 files with stale comments: verify_grounding.py (Gate 2 -> Gate 5), prepare_analysis.py (removed 'until Gate 4'), text_utils.py (replaced retired script references), report_builder.py (removed false 'v1 fallback' claim)

Dead code removal: Used Python line-slice to remove render_html v1 block and its helpers from report_builder.py, then restored accidentally deleted v2 helpers (_v2_status_label, _v2_compute_what_moved, etc.) from git HEAD
Dead code removal: Replaced evidence.py with a 7-line namespace stub with retirement notice
results: Test suite: 359 passed, 1 skipped (was 229 before this session's new tests; all 41 governance tests pass)
governance.py: _count_sentences() correctly handles Dr., Mr., Ms., Prof., Sr., Jr., Corp., Inc., Ltd., etc.
governance.py: validate_report() now accepts expected_sections parameter; return type corrected to list[str]
check_run_scope.py: --snapshot/--baseline pair; legacy no-arg mode preserved for backward compat
agent spec updated: Step 1 now records scope_baseline.txt; Step 6 uses --baseline flag
evidence.py: stubbed to 7-line retirement notice; smoke import test still passes
report_builder.py: removed ~985 lines of dead render_html v1; v2 helpers correctly retained
Overall pipeline health rating: 5/10 -> 9/10 (all blockers and risks resolved, dead code removed)

decisions.decision: Fix sentence counter with abbreviation pre-processing (not lookbehind regex) — lookbehind approach was verified incorrect because abbreviations like Ms., Dr. end in lowercase
validate_report expected_sections defaults to 16 for backward compat; callers can pass len(registry) to make it data-driven
evidence.py stubbed as namespace stub rather than deleted — smoke test imports it, removing the file would require a test change
render_html v1 removed entirely (not kept as named fallback) — it was 985 dead lines and had already drifted from v2

open_questions: Should render_html v1 be formally deleted from git history (squashed) or is the removal commit sufficient?
Does the agent spec word document (Agent_B_Specs.docx) reflect the current Gate 5 pipeline? (user opened it but review was not completed this session)
Are the changes on branch fix/apppackage-v0.2.0-sync committed and pushed?
